title: Adult/Child Choking (Conscious)
immediate_steps: Ask 'Are you choking?' and tell them you are going to help
Stand behind them and give 5 firm back blows between the shoulder blades using the heel of your hand
Give 5 abdominal thrusts (Heimlich maneuver): make a fist, place it just above the navel, grab it with your other hand, and pull inward and upward
Alternate 5 back blows and 5 abdominal thrusts until the object is forced out or the person becomes unconscious
avoid: Do not perform abdominal thrusts if they can still speak or cough forcefully (encourage them to cough instead)
Do not perform a blind finger sweep of the mouth

title: Infant Choking (Under 1 year)
immediate_steps: Hold the infant face down along your forearm, supporting their head and jaw
Give 5 firm back blows between the shoulder blades using the heel of your hand
Turn the infant face up on your forearm, supporting the head
Give 5 chest thrusts using two fingers in the center of the chest (pushing down about 1.5 inches)
Alternate 5 back blows and 5 chest thrusts until the object comes out or the infant goes unconscious
Call emergency services
avoid: Do not use abdominal thrusts (Heimlich maneuver) on an infant
Do not perform a blind finger sweep

title: Pregnant or Obese Person Choking
immediate_steps: Position yourself behind the person
Give 5 back blows between the shoulder blades
Instead of abdominal thrusts, perform Chest Thrusts
Place your arms under their armpits and encircle their chest
Place your fist on the center of their breastbone (sternum), pull straight back in a quick, sharp motion
Alternate back blows and chest thrusts
avoid: Do not perform abdominal thrusts on the belly

title: Choking Alone
immediate_steps: Call emergency services, leave the line open even if you can't speak
Perform the Heimlich maneuver on yourself: Make a fist, place it above your navel, grasp with your other hand, and push inward and upward
Alternatively, lean forcefully over a solid object like a chair back, table edge, or railing, and press your abdomen against it to force the object out
avoid: Do not go to a bathroom or hidden area (stay where someone might find you)

title: Choking - Recognition and Immediate Action
immediate_steps: Assess whether they can cough or speak
If they can cough forcefully, encourage them to continue coughing to expel the object
If they cannot breathe, speak, or cough effectively, immediate action is required to clear the airway
Call emergency help immediately while first aid is provided

title: Choking - Abdominal Thrust (Heimlich Maneuver)
immediate_steps: Stand behind the person and wrap your arms around their waist
Make a fist and place it just above the navel
Grasp the fist with the other hand and give quick inward and upward thrusts
Repeat until the object is expelled or the person becomes unconscious
Seek emergency medical help immediately
avoid: Do not apply pressure on the ribs or chest area

title: Choking - Back Blows and Special Cases
symptoms: Abdominal thrusts do not work or cannot be performed
immediate_steps: For general back blows: Lean the person forward slightly and give firm blows between the shoulder blades using the heel of your hand. Alternate between back blows and abdominal thrusts
For infants: Use a combination of back blows and chest thrusts carefully
For pregnant or obese individuals: Use chest thrusts instead of abdominal thrusts
avoid: Do not use abdominal thrusts on infants, pregnant women, or obese individuals

title: Choking - Unconscious Person and Recovery
immediate_steps: Gently lower them to the ground and call emergency services immediately
Begin CPR by starting chest compressions to attempt to dislodge the obstruction
Before giving rescue breaths, check the mouth for any visible object and remove it carefully if accessible
Continue CPR until the object is expelled or medical help arrives
After recovery, the person must be medically evaluated even if breathing returns to normal
